records.summary: StaticEvaluator is the ECMAScript evaluator frontier for world construction, configuration, and explicit open seams.
records.rationale: Evaluator gaps should usually trigger low-level substrate improvement, not local smoke-test shims. Keep this as the first reuse point when a feature needs module variables, imports, static closures, or evaluator policy.
guidance: Prefer extending evaluator capabilities and open-seam modeling over adding feature-local evaluator shortcuts.

StaticEvaluationExpressionReader should keep one evaluator per reader, matching RuntimeBindingSourceEvaluationFrame's per-source evaluator lifetime, so guardrails and seam checkpoints do not reset for every follow-up property, target, or expression probe.
When a gap appears, also check whether Atlas needs better flow or capability projection to expose the evaluator route.
Use `framework:evaluator` for targeted framework evaluator effects, open seams, and module summaries before expanding semantic-runtime evaluator/world-construction support.
`framework:evaluator --memberName=...` narrows the traced root; `--calleeName=...` only filters returned effects. Use memberName when checking whether one framework method closes without open seams.
Keep evaluator policy explicit; do not bury dynamic-runtime uncertainty in defensive fallbacks.

Atlas framework effect tracing treats return, throw, break, and continue as block-exit completions; if unsupported-statement seams reappear for ordinary control flow, fix the tracer instead of filtering them out downstream.

StaticEvaluator handles exact switch fallthrough when the discriminant and case expressions close statically. Unknown or boundary case selection should remain a dynamic-branch seam, not speculative multi-branch execution.

StaticEvaluator handles closed try/catch/finally completion flow. Catch bindings are temporary evaluator bindings and shadowing an existing binding remains an explicit unsupported-binding-pattern seam until nested lexical catch environments are modeled.

Atlas framework effect tracing now binds destructured variable, parameter, callback, and for-of names as lexical flow facts with destructured paths; if ordinary object/array destructuring reappears as an unsupported-binding-pattern seam, improve the tracer rather than filtering that seam downstream.

Guardrails belong on StaticEvaluationPolicy; speculative intrinsic attempts should checkpoint/restore seams and guardrail counters so abandoned callback evaluation degrades to imprecise values instead of poisoning the module.

Bounded classic `for` loops with evaluator-known conditions and incrementors belong in StaticEvaluator alongside for-of/for-in. Simple numeric/string compound assignments and ++/-- update expressions should spend the shared assignment-target and operator reducers before becoming dynamic-mutation seams.

Use IntrinsicCallbackFrame for callback-bearing standard intrinsics so callback budget, checkpoint lifetime, and abandoned-seam restoration remain one evaluator primitive instead of per-method guardrail loops.

Collection callback budget exhaustion should usually return imprecise evaluator values such as unknown membership/order; emit product-specific open seams later only when a consumer needs exact membership or order.

Standard object/array/string/promise intrinsics such as Object.keys/values/entries/fromEntries, Array.from, array map/filter/find/findIndex/some/every/reduce/flat/flatMap/forEach/includes/indexOf/join/sort, array push/pop/shift/unshift/splice/reverse/fill mutation, string split/replace/replaceAll/charAt/charCodeAt/at/substring/repeat, and Promise.resolve belong behind evaluation/intrinsics.ts, with implementations split under evaluation/intrinsics/ by ECMAScript family and shared callback guardrails.

Static evaluator intrinsics are not a parallel TypeScript standard library. Add standard behavior only when a semantic-runtime consumer needs deterministic source-value closure; TypeScript remains the authority for checker-backed stdlib declarations, generics, overloads, and library member surfaces.

Date construction follows the same intrinsic boundary. `new Date('YYYY-MM-DD')`, timezone-qualified ISO strings, and numeric epochs can close as evaluator-local Date values for app-world/source-value consumers; `new Date()`, local-time constructor overloads, and arbitrary host parsing stay runtime-open.

Mutating array intrinsics must update the evaluator-local receiver and carry membership/order imprecision on EvaluationArrayValue; do not return detached arrays or add feature-local array mutation shortcuts in recognizers.

EvaluationArrayValue carries compact membership/order imprecision plus evaluator-local `uncertainties` for boundary spreads, conditional branches, elision holes, non-array spreads, and unknown ordering. The empty uncertainty case shares a singleton so exact arrays stay cheap; consumers that need public explanations such as `dependencies` being open because an `import.meta.env` conditional spread controlled optional entries should spend those evaluator-owned uncertainties instead of guessing from syntax or MCP display text.

Binding patterns belong in evaluator environment construction. Variable declarations, function/constructor parameters, defaults, rest bindings, and loop declaration initializers should bind environment cells before recognizers inspect evaluated values.

Property and element access belong in evaluation/property-access.ts. Reuse readStaticOwnProperty, writeStaticOwnProperty, evaluateStaticPropertyValue/evaluateStaticElementValue, and the host-neutral readStaticValueProperty/readStaticValueElement helpers for recognizers, binding-source reads, or follow-up expression reads instead of duplicating object/module/array/string/RegExp/getter access semantics.

Array prototype boundary recognition in evaluation/property-access.ts should spend expression/array-method-semantics.ts rather than maintaining a second native Array method list; the descriptor is the joined authority for TypeChecker projection, source-value reduction, connectable observation, and static host prototype boundaries.

String prototype boundary recognition in evaluation/property-access.ts should spend staticStringPrototypeBoundaryMethods from evaluation/intrinsics/string-intrinsics.ts rather than carrying another host string method list.

Declaration instantiation belongs in evaluation/declaration-instantiation.ts. Module and block import/function setup plus class lexical-cell declaration should stay ahead of statement execution and should not be duplicated in module graph or recognizer code. Do not evaluate static class properties during declaration instantiation; they execute with the class declaration after prior module statements have initialized their bindings.

The evaluator module graph is runtime-shaped. Type-only imports and type-only re-exports should be erased before graph evaluation so TypeScript-only cycles do not become artificial runtime import cycles during DI/source-value activation.

CommonJS carrier semantics belong in evaluation/commonjs.ts. StaticEvaluator materializes authored exports/module carriers there and StaticModuleGraphEvaluator reads export/require values through the same substrate.

Class and function value execution belong in evaluation/class-values.ts and evaluation/function-values.ts. Keep class lifecycle and call-frame construction there while preserving StaticEvaluator as the owner of recursion, guardrails, and open seams.

Aurelia runtime-host intrinsics may ask StaticIntrinsicEvaluationHost for generic class instantiation, but product-specific calls such as ambient resolve(ClassKey) should stay at the host boundary and only close in activation-like frames with `this`; `evaluateAureliaResolveCall(...)` owns that narrow path through `aureliaResolveDirectKey(...)` and `evaluateAureliaResolveDirectClassKey(...)`. Registered/interface DI keys join through RuntimeBindingSourceActivationContext.resolveCallFrame(...) and active-container resolver lookup when a modeled container is available, not through the generic evaluator.

Browser ambient globals such as document, window, self, customElements, and console are Aurelia host-runtime boundary values during app admission. Keep them in aureliaStaticEvaluationRuntimeHost so app setup expressions remain host-dependent boundaries instead of unresolved-identifier seams, and keep ordinary host-boundary method calls/property writes such as animation-frame, storage, keyboard event, WebGL, and AudioContext usage as boundary propagation rather than generic dynamic-call/dynamic-mutation seam sites.

Effective TypeScript lib ambient value declarations and project-local `.d.ts` ambient `declare const/let/var` globals, including `declare global` module declarations, are host-environment boundary values for static evaluation. Keep this in StaticProjectEvaluationPass via ambient-globals.ts so browser/stdlib names and Vite/build-time injected variables stop producing unresolved-identifier seams without pretending their literal values are statically known.

`import.meta` is a host-environment boundary object. `import.meta.env.*` should be legal evaluator syntax that carries host/bundler dependency forward to consumers such as resource convergence without becoming an evaluator unsupported-expression seam.

Evaluator open-seam reason kinds are producer-owned. `EvaluationOpenSeam` now carries compact producer reason kinds through `EvaluationKernelEmitter` into kernel open seams for core evaluator lanes such as missing identifiers, unsupported loops, and unsupported compound assignments; expand producer threading at the seam creation point rather than parsing summaries or stamping broad display heuristics.

Primitive operator value algebra belongs in evaluation/operators.ts instead of bottom-of-evaluator helper islands. Loose == and != must model primitive/nullish ECMAScript equality instead of aliasing strict equality; this keeps common guards such as value == null aligned across StaticEvaluator and binding-source value reads.

Relational operator reduction should close the deterministic numeric and string comparison lanes shared by StaticEvaluator and binding-source source-value callbacks. Leave mixed coercion or host-object comparison open until a consumer proves that broader ECMAScript coercion subset is needed.

Representative evaluator values belong in evaluation/representative-values.ts. Repeated views and speculative conditionals should share the same conservative summary policy: unanimous exact values stay exact, string-like branches become string patterns, common object fields survive, and unrelated lanes become binding-scope boundaries.

anchors.summary: Static ECMAScript evaluator implementation.
ECMAScript declaration-instantiation substrate for imports, function hoists, and class lexical cells.
Focused contract for static class field evaluation after prior module const initialization.
Shared CommonJS carrier materialization and export/require reads.
Static class property, instance property, parameter-property, and constructor-execution substrate.
Evaluator-local function call-frame construction, async boundary, and completion-to-value substrate.
Pure primitive operator value algebra for the static evaluator.
Shared conservative representative-value merge substrate for repeat locals and speculative conditional branches.
ECMAScript-shaped property/element access, own property read/write, and property receiver semantics.
Expression-reading helper adjacent to evaluator expansion.
Aurelia-specific static-evaluation host boundary for browser globals, app admission, and narrow ambient resolve(ClassKey) closure.
Effective TypeScript lib and project-local `.d.ts` ambient global reader for host/browser/build-time values exposed as host-environment boundaries.
Bridge from evaluator boundary values to open-seam reason vocabulary.
Evaluator-local seam carrier now preserves producer reason kinds until kernel publication.
Default evaluator seam-kind to open-seam-reason mapping used before producer-specific reasons are compacted.
Contract proving evaluator-produced open seams carry reason kinds for missing identifiers, unsupported loops, and compound assignments.
Contract proving bounded classic for loops and simple compound/update assignments reduce through StaticEvaluator guardrails.
Contract proving import.meta.env reduces to host-environment boundaries without evaluator syntax seams.
Contract proving standard globals and deterministic string/array intrinsics close without unresolved property seams.

Contract proving declared ambient/browser globals, browser-game host-boundary operations, and deterministic simulation helpers do not become unresolved identifiers or generic dynamic-call/dynamic-mutation seams while missing globals remain source-filterable.
Host-bound ambient resolve(...) evaluator path for activation-like direct class-key calls.
Direct-key admission for evaluator-local ambient resolve(...), leaving wrappers and non-direct key shapes open.
Evaluator-local class-key instantiation path for direct ambient resolve(ClassKey).
Binding-source DI activation context for registered/interface resolve(...) keys when a modeled active container is available.
Binding-source activation resolve(...) frame for registered/interface key admission before active-container resolver lookup.
Standard intrinsic dispatch boundary imported by StaticEvaluator.
Array intrinsic implementation family with callback guardrails.
Promise intrinsic implementation family, including fulfillment-lane preservation.
String intrinsic implementation family, including boundary method recognition and deterministic charAt/charCodeAt/at/substring/repeat closure.
Shared intrinsic boundary/coercion/slice helpers and IntrinsicCallbackFrame callback guardrail lifetime.
Environment binding pattern materialization for variables, parameters, defaults, rest, and loop declaration initializers.
Binding-name collection used for temporary lexical catch binding lifetime.
Atlas framework evaluator effect tracing, including block-exit completion flow for return/throw/break/continue.
Queryable framework.evaluator CLI for method-root static invocation effects and evaluator open seams.
Framework evaluator effect surface for comparison.

records.summary: StaticModuleGraphEvaluator owns local module evaluation order, import/export linkage, CommonJS exports, re-exports, and external boundary values.
records.rationale: Feature pressure that needs imported values, plugin registry bodies, source package linking, or module namespaces should reuse the module graph evaluator instead of invoking StaticEvaluator over isolated files. This class is the boundary where ECMAScript module linking becomes evaluator-local values.
guidance: Start here when a feature needs static import values, re-export resolution, namespace imports, CommonJS carriers, or dynamic import boundary propagation.

Keep type-only imports and type-only re-exports out of runtime import/export linkage. If a local imported class opens only through a type-only cycle, fix module-graph admission before weakening downstream composition, DI, router, or authoring expectations.

Use `contract:evaluation-module-graph` when changing import/export admission, especially around `import type`, type-only export declarations, mixed value/type specifiers, and runtime TypeScript declarations such as enums.

Local side-effect imports are module execution edges. If resource/configuration/router pressure sees an admitted source file but no evaluated products from it, check `evaluateSideEffectImport` before adding feature-local discovery.
Keep Aurelia-specific ownership in evaluation policy, runtimeHost, or downstream recognizers; the module graph evaluator should stay ECMAScript-shaped.
If external app analysis loses product-specific intrinsics during follow-up expression reads, check that callers reuse this graph result and runtime host.

anchors.summary: Module graph evaluator with import/export linkage.
Runtime-shaped import/export graph reader that erases type-only imports/re-exports.
Local side-effect import execution edge for modules that produce no import binding.
Static evaluation module graph and expression-reader guidance.
Focused runtime-shaped import/export graph contract for type-only edge erasure and value-edge preservation.

records.summary: FileSystemEvaluationModuleSourceHost is the source-resolution boundary for recursive evaluator module graph construction.
records.rationale: External app and plugin pressure depends on resolving authored TS/JS, local JSON/HTML/CSS assets, emitted JS specifiers, path aliases, and package declarations that can map back to source. This host should stay the single module-source admission point instead of growing separate package/source readers in recognizers.
guidance: Start here when local asset modules, package declaration-to-source mapping, path aliases, or module graph admission rules change.
Reuse the shared boot host-file cache for package manifest reads; do not add independent manifest readers inside feature code.
Keep Aurelia framework packages on the framework-emulation path rather than pulling `aurelia` or `@aurelia/*` from node_modules into app evaluation.

Module graph performance should be read through the source-host profile first: source reads/parses, TypeScript module resolution, path probes, declaration-source cache, package-policy cache, and cached file-system probes are separate trade-off lanes.

Use StaticProjectEvaluationProfile source-file composition before changing package-source admission. The profile distinguishes project, node_modules, external, TS/JS, asset, evaluated, open, and source-text buckets so a large graph can be attributed before refactoring.

Source-shipped package entrypoint admission is a product/app-analysis policy, not the generic evaluator default. Aurelia project evaluation may let bare package imports reach TypeScript/source admission when a package manifest points at authored source such as `src/index.ts`; opaque built package entrypoints should remain external-module boundaries with public reason-coded seams.

Direct path probes are allowed for asset/query-shaped imports such as JSON/HTML/CSS and query-bearing specifiers. Ordinary TS/JS relative imports should let TypeScript choose source/declaration semantics first. The measured post-TypeScript path-probe retry is a deliberate `EvaluationModuleResolutionPolicy` fallback for relative specifiers whose local authored source or asset shape TypeScript did not close; read `postTsProbe`, `pathProbeBefore/After`, before/after probe milliseconds, `resolvedByPathProbeBefore/After`, and the specifier-shape counters before changing it.

Keep path-probe candidate policy semantic, not exhaustive. Explicit supported asset/source extensions are exact candidates and should not expand into `specifier.ext/index.*`; emitted JavaScript extensions can map back to TypeScript source siblings; extensionless specifiers expand through supported evaluation-module extensions and index files.

When TypeScript has already resolved a non-declaration module to an evaluation-module path, do not re-probe file existence before admitting it. Existence checks belong in candidate path probes or TypeScript's resolver, not in a duplicate post-resolution guard.

Use cached read results as the source-file miss test. `readSourceFile` should not probe file existence and then read the same path; resolver/path-probe code owns existence checks when it is choosing among candidate paths.

anchors.summary: File-system implementation of the evaluation module source host.
Per-pass cached filesystem adapter and profile counters for module graph source reads and resolution.
Watchpoints for package and module-source resolution.
Contract proving app-world analysis admits source-shipped package entrypoints while opaque built packages stay reason-coded external-module boundaries.

records.summary: StaticProjectEvaluationFrame owns the per-run project evaluation lifetime behind the shared StaticProjectEvaluationPass facade.
records.rationale: Resource, configuration, DI, template, and type-system work need one shared evaluation envelope over app sources and linked source dependencies. StaticProjectEvaluationPass should stay a small public entry point; StaticProjectEvaluationFrame owns the per-run admission index, module source host, graph evaluation, result publication, and profile assembly. Project-level evaluation should remain evaluator substrate and kernel open-seam emission, not an Aurelia recognizer.
guidance: Start at StaticProjectEvaluationFrame when app-source admission, linked source admission, unresolved module capture, phase timing, module-source-host profiling, or evaluator open-seam emission changes.
Keep StaticProjectEvaluationPass as a small facade over the per-run frame unless a new public evaluation mode needs a distinct entry point.
Keep source roles authoritative: tests, declarations, templates, styles, manifests, tooling scripts, and tooling configs should not enter app-world static evaluation by accident.

Keep checker/project inclusion separate from Aurelia app-world evaluation eligibility. Top-level TS/JS tooling scripts, including `.mjs`, are `tooling-script` sources: they remain visible to ordinary TypeScript diagnostics without producing app-world static evaluation seams. Do not force them through `app-source` merely to keep checker visibility.

Use the source composition profile to separate app-authored sources from linked source-shipped packages before deciding whether a broad evaluated-source count is an admission bug or legitimate framework/plugin pressure.

Static project evaluation source results carry compact source-origin contributions. Preserve both `static-evaluation-root` and `module-graph-dependency` when a source is directly discovered as app-source and also reached through startup/import graph evaluation.
Public open-seam context should consume those source origins query-time instead of adding durable kernel records or MCP-local source-origin guesses.
If a downstream materializer needs another evaluation loop, inspect whether it should consume StaticProjectEvaluationResult instead.

anchors.summary: Public project-level static evaluation facade.
Compact in-memory source-origin contribution row for direct static-evaluation roots and module graph dependencies.
Per-run project evaluation work frame and profile assembly owner.
Boot source-role classifier that separates top-level tooling scripts from app-world sources.
Top-level scripts/tools/tooling TS/JS/.mjs classifier for diagnostics-visible tooling scripts that should not enter app-world evaluation.
Source-role enum containing ToolingScript for build or maintenance scripts that need checker diagnostics but not app-world evaluation.
Checker-owned source role bridge that should remain compatible with future project-inclusion versus app-world-evaluation split.
Contract proving top-level tooling scripts, including `.mjs`, keep TypeScript diagnostics while staying out of app-world open-seam sites.
Project-level evaluation envelope notes.

records.summary: ModuleLoader is the framework-shaped evaluator service for kernel module transform-input semantics and AUR0021 diagnostics.
records.rationale: The generic evaluator should stay ECMAScript-shaped, but Aurelia's IModuleLoader is itself an evaluation handoff. ModuleLoader issue materialization should spend invalid_module_transform_input only after evaluating source inputs, and downstream registry/resource materializers should handle analyzed module exports instead of duplicating transform checks.
guidance: Start here when `aliasedResourcesRegistry(...)`, `IModuleLoader.load(...)`, or analyzed module export pressure appears.

Keep direct-input versus promise-fulfillment behavior aligned with `kernel:ModuleLoader`: direct primitive/function/nullish inputs reject, promise nullish fulfillments reject, and non-null non-object promise fulfillments analyze to an empty module.
ModuleLoaderIssueMaterializer scans evaluated project sources, so issue source spans must use the EvaluatedProjectSource admission handle rather than re-resolving the source file by name.
Use EvaluationIssuePublisher for evaluation diagnostics so issue evidence, provenance, identity, and materialized-product records stay behind the shared kernel issue-publication primitive.
Do not suppress `di.open-registry-body` for aliased registries with a shim; the honest next layer is resource-export and registry-body materialization from analyzed module items.

RegistryBodyReference now distinguishes framework-owned IRegistry body shape from framework-registration bundles; `aliasedResourcesRegistry(...)` closes only when module input and alias parameters are statically interpreted.

Generated body admissions currently materialize resource-definition module items with ResourceDefinition.register(container, alias) lookup-name overrides; non-resource module items remain explicit registration strategy pressure.

anchors.summary: Semantic-runtime mirror of the kernel module loader transform semantics.
Framework-shaped evaluator service for module transform input analysis.
Source-backed issue materializer for aliasedResourcesRegistry and IModuleLoader.load calls.
Evaluation-specific issue publisher over the shared kernel issue-publication primitive.
Registration value reference for framework-owned IRegistry body semantics such as aliasedResourcesRegistry.
Configuration materialization handoff from analyzed module items into resource registration admissions.
Exact framework-error link check for AUR0021.

records.summary: FrameworkApiIssueMaterializer owns exact source-local framework API diagnostics for kernel utilities and metadata calls.
records.rationale: Some framework errors are not DI, resource, template, or configuration behavior; they are source-visible utility/API guards. They still belong below higher Aurelia recognizers because TypeChecker/import grounding can prove the same framework branch without running user code.
guidance: Start here when framework ErrorNames or raw Error rows point at direct source API calls rather than container, registration, resource, template, routing, or validation semantics.

Keep claims source-local and conservative: EventAggregator publish/subscribe requires a framework-grounded receiver plus a statically falsy first argument; firstDefined requires no arguments or all statically undefined arguments; Metadata.define requires a framework-imported Metadata receiver and statically empty metadata keys.
Use frameworkRawErrorAuthority for exact raw framework API guards such as Metadata.define-without-key instead of inventing AUR labels.
Framework API issue spans should carry the boot-admitted source-file handle through sourceSpanAddressForSite; do not re-resolve scanner-owned locations from filenames.
Publish framework API issue records through EvaluationIssuePublisher instead of assembling evidence/provenance/identity/product records locally.
Do not make the generic StaticEvaluator recognize Aurelia framework APIs unless the framework behavior is actually an evaluator service like ModuleLoader.

anchors.summary: Source-backed issue materializer for framework utility/API calls.
Shared evaluation issue product publisher.
Framework error-code grounding for EventAggregator source API guards.
Framework error-code grounding for firstDefined.
Exact raw Error authority grounding for Metadata.define without a key.

records.summary: ModuleEnvironmentRecord is the ECMAScript-like binding-cell model for module and evaluator-local function environments.
records.rationale: Environment records are evaluator machinery, not durable kernel products, but they are central to correct static execution: declaration, initialization, assignment, open state, CommonJS carriers, and cloned branch/function frames all share this model.
guidance: Start here when lexical binding state, assignment mutability, CommonJS carriers, function-call frames, or branch clones change.
Keep evaluator-local binding cells out of durable kernel records unless a specific fact earns a product identity.
Do not replace environment lookup with ad hoc maps inside expression or module evaluators.

anchors.summary: Evaluator-local ECMAScript-like environment record.
One binding cell inside an environment record.
Module environment responsibilities.
